_maintenanceNotes.legacyFields: pc, mobile, hide, and RecSee-only customContent are still used by the current React components. shortDescription is used by the slide hero. description is not rendered and can be removed after useful text is migrated into details.summary.

projects.title: BSideU
projects.shortDescription: A full-stack collaboration platform for discovering side projects, recruiting teammates, and managing project activity.

details.summary: BSideU is a full-stack side-project collaboration platform where users can discover project ideas, recruit developers, designers, and project managers, save projects, like posts, comment on discussions, and manage their own project postings. The frontend delivers a responsive Expo web experience connected to a GraphQL backend with authenticated and guest browsing, OAuth-based sessions, project filtering, recommendation data, profile management, and media upload support.

role.title: Full Stack Development
role.description: I implemented both the GraphQL backend and the Expo/React Native frontend. On the frontend, I built the project discovery interface, responsive project cards, search and filter flows, multi-step project posting and editing forms, project detail pages, comments, likes, account management, authenticated route handling, and GraphQL data integration. On the backend, I built the NestJS API, authentication, OAuth flows, project CRUD, comments, likes, S3 presigned uploads, validation, query optimization, and tests.

features.label: Project Discovery
features.title: Searchable and filterable project listing
features.description: Built a responsive project browsing experience with keyword search, pagination, recruiting status, position, location, and interest filters. Logged-in users also see personalized recommendation sections while guests can still browse public project data.

features.label: Project Cards
features.title: Scannable recruiting information
features.description: Designed project cards that summarize industry, timezone, collaboration type, open roles, deadline status, author profile, views, comments, and likes in a compact layout that works across desktop and mobile widths.

features.label: Project Posting
features.title: Multi-step project creation and editing
features.description: Implemented a four-step form for industry, dates, recruiting roles, collaboration details, title, description, and contact confirmation. React Hook Form and Zod validation keep text-heavy project requirements reliable before submission.

features.label: Project Detail
features.title: Project detail, ownership, and sharing flows
features.description: Built the detail screen with author metadata, formatted project dates, project content sections, role information cards, likes, comments, sharing, edit access, and owner-only project deletion through the backend hide-project mutation.

features.label: Engagement
features.title: Likes and comment discussions
features.description: Connected like and comment interactions to GraphQL mutations and queries, including optimistic UI updates, rollback behavior on failed likes, comment refetching, and immediate local removal for deleted comments.

features.label: Account
features.title: Saved projects and profile management
features.description: Created my-page flows for saved projects, created projects, and account settings. Users can update profile fields such as name, position, experience, interests, and profile image data through GraphQL mutations.

features.label: Backend API
features.title: GraphQL API for collaboration workflows
features.description: Built the NestJS backend with GraphQL resolvers, TypeORM repositories, PostgreSQL persistence, OAuth login, JWT sessions, project filtering, recommendations, comments, likes, S3 presigned uploads, email workflows, and DataLoader-based query optimization.

features.label: Responsive Experience
features.title: Expo web interface for desktop and mobile
features.description: Used Expo Router, React Native Web, NativeWind, reusable page containers, responsive utility classes, accessible labels, and shared UI components to support the same product flows across desktop and mobile web.

details.challenge: The main challenge was turning a text-heavy recruiting workflow into a clear product experience while keeping the full-stack data model consistent. The interface needed to make projects easy to scan, support guest and authenticated states, preserve reliable form validation, and stay in sync with GraphQL data for recommendations, filters, likes, comments, saved projects, and account updates.
process: Mapped the main user journeys for browsing, filtering, viewing details, posting projects, editing projects, saving projects, and managing account information.
Built reusable frontend components such as project cards, section filters, pagination, form steps, dropdowns, modals, avatars, buttons, and shared page containers.
Connected screens to GraphQL queries and mutations with URQL, Jotai auth state, protected route wrappers, optimistic like behavior, and refetch flows for comments and project updates.
Translated backend project fields into frontend-friendly layouts for recruiting roles, deadlines, timezone-aware dates, locations, contact information, and project status.
Implemented backend modules for authentication, OAuth, users, projects, comments, uploads, mail, industries, options, DataLoaders, validation, and unit-tested service behavior.
Reviewed the responsive Expo web layout across desktop and mobile-oriented widths, keeping dense project information scannable without hiding core actions.

projects.title: Para Siempre
projects.shortDescription: A serverless wedding platform with RSVP workflows, admin operations, configurable media permissions, and responsive gallery delivery.

details.summary: Para Siempre is a serverless wedding platform built to centralize event information, RSVP collection, guest communication, and post-event media sharing. Guests can review wedding details, browse the timeline, submit RSVP information, answer conditional questionnaire prompts, and upload photos or videos. The platform also includes a protected admin experience for reviewing RSVP records, tracking attendance, managing gallery permissions, and sending follow-up emails, backed by Firebase Cloud Functions, Firestore, Storage, and responsive image processing.

role.title: Frontend-Led Full Stack Engineer
role.description: I designed and built the React guest experience, authenticated admin dashboard, Firebase data flows, Cloud Functions, Storage upload pipeline, responsive media delivery, and Mailgun email automation.

features.label: RSVP System
features.title: Structured RSVP and conditional guest flow
features.description: Built a custom RSVP flow that captures guest identity, email validation, guest count, and conditional questionnaire responses such as travel origin, transportation needs, and contact information. The flow stores submissions through Firebase callable functions, supports updates to existing records, and avoids duplicate RSVP submissions by email.

features.label: Admin Dashboard
features.title: Operations Dashboard for Guest and Gallery Management
features.description: Expanded the authenticated admin dashboard into an operations surface with RSVP records, attendance metrics, confirmation tracking, inline questionnaire review, check-in controls, and gallery policy management. Admins can monitor guest counts, mark arrivals, send confirmation emails, trigger follow-up campaigns, and change gallery upload and save settings without touching backend code.

features.label: Media Sharing
features.title: Controlled Guest Upload Pipeline
features.description: Built a media upload workflow with local previews, duplicate-file filtering, removable selections, and signed Firebase Storage uploads. The backend supports configurable upload permissions, allowing uploads to remain public or be restricted to checked-in attendees.

features.label: Gallery Experience
features.title: Responsive Media Gallery Delivery
features.description: Upgraded the gallery to return thumbnails and responsive srcSet metadata for uploaded images, reducing heavy full-size image loading on the main grid. Cloud Functions generate WebP variants on upload and support backfilling older media, while the frontend preserves fullscreen browsing and admin delete controls.

Upgraded the gallery to return thumbnails and responsive srcSet metadata for uploaded images, reducing heavy full-size image loading on the main grid. Cloud Functions generate WebP variants on upload and support backfilling older media, while the frontend preserves fullscreen browsing and admin delete controls.

features.label: Media Governance
features.title: Configurable Gallery Access Controls
features.description: Implemented admin-managed media policies for upload eligibility and browser save behavior. This added a lightweight content-governance layer to the gallery without introducing a separate custom server.

features.label: Event Information
features.title: Mobile-Friendly Event Details
features.description: Designed responsive event sections for date, location, embedded Google Maps, mobile map app handoff, and ceremony timeline content so guests could quickly access essential information before and during the event.

features.label: Email Automation
features.title: Transactional Guest Communication
features.description: Integrated Mailgun with Firebase Cloud Functions to send RSVP confirmations, reminders, and post-event follow-up emails. Email templates include personalized guest details, event information, questionnaire answers, and media upload links.

details.challenge: The main challenge was evolving a personal event site into a lightweight operations platform without adding traditional server infrastructure. The product needed to remain simple for guests on mobile while supporting admin workflows such as RSVP tracking, check-in state, follow-up communication, media moderation, configurable upload permissions, and efficient gallery delivery.
process: Mapped the guest journey into separate flows for event discovery, RSVP submission, confirmation, gallery browsing, and media upload.
Used reusable React components for form fields, buttons, dropdowns, questionnaire steps, media cards, modal viewing, and admin panels to keep the UI consistent across public and admin pages.

Modeled backend behavior with Firebase callable functions so RSVP writes, email actions, media listing, upload permissions, gallery settings, and protected deletes could be handled securely outside the client.
Implemented Firebase Auth and protected routes for admin-only access while keeping the public guest experience frictionless.
Used Firestore as the RSVP source of truth and Firebase Storage as the media layer, with callable Cloud Functions enforcing upload rules, admin mutations, and gallery settings.
Added responsive image generation and metadata-driven gallery delivery to improve performance for photo-heavy views while keeping the implementation fully serverless.

details.summary: Furence is a company website designed to present company information, job postings, news, and office updates in a clear and accessible format.

role.title: UI Design & Development
role.description: I was responsible for the visual design and the technical build of the user interface. My work involved creating a clean layout that organizes dense data into a readable format and turning wireframes into functional code.

features.label: Main Feature
features.title: Job posting system
features.description: A structured area for sharing hiring information and open roles.

features.label: Main Feature
features.title: Centralized notice system
features.description: A structured news feed that allows the company to share office updates, technical documentation, and event announcements.

features.label: Responsive Design
features.title: Mobile-friendly access to key content
features.description: The interface is built to adapt to any screen size, making it easy for staff to check information on large office monitors or mobile phones.

features.label: Desktop Experience
features.title: Readable company website structure
features.description: The desktop layout organizes company information, news, and service content into a clear page structure.

details.challenge: The main challenge was organizing company updates, job information, and technical content without making the interface feel crowded.
process: Reviewed existing company content and grouped it by user intent.
Designed layouts that made news, jobs, and company information easier to scan.
Built responsive UI components and tested desktop and mobile layouts.

projects.title: Perry & Sherry

details.summary: Perry & Sherry is a wedding RSVP website that presents the couple's story, event location, and guest response flow in one web experience.

role.title: Web Design & Development
role.description: I designed and developed the wedding website, translating event information into a responsive and personal web experience.

features.label: Story
features.title: Couple story section
features.description: A dedicated story page introduces the couple and sets the tone for the wedding invitation.

features.label: Location
features.title: Event location details
features.description: The location section gives guests quick access to venue details and travel context.

features.label: RSVP
features.title: Guest response form
features.description: The RSVP screen helps guests send their response through the website.

details.challenge: The main challenge was balancing personal storytelling with practical event information in a layout guests could scan quickly.
process: Organized content around the main guest needs: story, location, and RSVP.
Designed pages with a consistent visual tone for the wedding.
Built the website as a responsive React experience and deployed it for guest access.

details.summary: ITSM is a dashboard application concept for managing service information, tickets, calendar views, reports, and settings.

details.summary: Landing Page is a company landing page focused on presenting the brand, main message, and supporting content in a single long-form page.
